meaning: adj.可能发生的,附随的,暂时的n.偶然的事情,分遣队
examples: The crowd included a big contingent from Cleveland.
The American contingent will stay overnight in London.
The contingent marched in the direction of the Western Hills.
Its value, in other words, is largely contingent on what someone else is willing to pay for it.
Disney CEO Robert Iger and ESPN chief George Bodenheimer will lead the ESPN contingent.
